detail: GWAS Catalog association with p=3e-9. Initial sample: 56,705 European ancestry heavy and light drinkers, 9,725 African American or Afro-Caribbean heavy and light drinkers, 1,789 Asian ancestry heavy and light drinkers, 4,812 Hispanic or Latin American heavy and light drinkers. Replication sample: 42,077 European ancestry heavy drinkers, 217 African American heavy drinkers, 282,294 Asian ancestry heavy drinkers, 124 Hispanic ancestry heavy drinkers, 74,701 European ancestry light drinkers, 845 African American light drinkers, 24,129 Asian ancestry light drinkers, 411 Hispanic light drinkers.
whyItMatters: This common-variant association is matched locally against the uploaded genotype and should be treated as a tendency signal, not a prediction.
conditions: Mean arterial pressure x alcohol consumption (light vs heavy) interaction (2df test)
gwasInitialSampleSize: 56,705 European ancestry heavy and light drinkers, 9,725 African American or Afro-Caribbean heavy and light drinkers, 1,789 Asian ancestry heavy and light drinkers, 4,812 Hispanic or Latin American heavy and light drinkers
gwasReplicationSampleSize: 42,077 European ancestry heavy drinkers, 217 African American heavy drinkers, 282,294 Asian ancestry heavy drinkers, 124 Hispanic ancestry heavy drinkers, 74,701 European ancestry light drinkers, 845 African American light drinkers, 24,129 Asian ancestry light drinkers, 411 Hispanic light drinkers

title: CFTR / ivacaftor
summary: Patients with the rs121909020 AA genotype (two copies of the CFTR A1067T variant) and cystic fibrosis may respond to ivacaftor treatment. FDA-approved drug labeling information and CPIC guidelines indicate use of ivacaftor in cystic fibrosis patients with at least one copy of A1067T. Other genetic and clinical factors may also influence response to ivacaftor.
riskSummary: CFTR variant associated with efficacy for ivacaftor
detail: Patients with the rs121909020 AA genotype (two copies of the CFTR A1067T variant) and cystic fibrosis may respond to ivacaftor treatment. FDA-approved drug labeling information and CPIC guidelines indicate use of ivacaftor in cystic fibrosis patients with at least one copy of A1067T. Other genetic and clinical factors may also influence response to ivacaftor.
whyItMatters: PharmGKB curates pharmacogenomic evidence from the literature, with level 1A–2B annotations backed by expert review and published clinical studies.
notes: PharmGKB annotations reflect published literature and should not replace clinical pharmacogenomic testing.

title: CFTR / ivacaftor
